content.full_text:
April 21, 1992


Deanne Stone
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California    95804

Dear Ms. Stone:

In reference to our phone call of April 20, 1992, I am sending you the information in writing and requesting that a legal opinion be sent to me for a future decision that affects certain rules being proposed in Mendocino County.

The Mendocino County Board of Supervisors will be reviewing a proposal brought to us by a special Timber Advisory Committee to make certain changes in timber production in Mendocino County. The proposal reduces the harvestable amounts on private lands in Mendocino County.  It contains a formula for industrial lands of over five acres to 2% of the growth on those lands; and for the ownerships of 5000 acres and under would adopt rules that are in effect in Santa Cruz County.   These changes would be more restrictive than State law.   The Mendocino County Board of Supervisors would adopt the changes and then recommend them to the State Board of Forestry for final adoption, and then a public hearing would follow conducted by the State.

My Mother's property would be subject to the Santa Cruz rules, or could possibly be subject to the 2% rule if the acreage size should be reduced to include smaller properties.

My two sisters and I at present are heirs to the properties in her ownership.   We have at present a revocable trust or living trust that covers all properties owned by her.

I receive no income or any compensation from her for my labor or time spent on the ranch.  We operated the ranch as a partnership before my coming to work as a board member in 1974.  I received a limited income from the ranch and supplemental income from equipment work done on neighboring properties in the community.  After becoming a Board member the Partnership dissolved due to

Deanne Stone
Page 2
4/21/92

the time devoted to my new occupation.  We reduced the activities on the ranch to maintain the buildings, houses, fences and improvements, at the same time involving ourselves in raising livestock, but in smaller numbers.

The timber activities were conducted on the properties in 1959-62, and until recently there has been nothing done with that asset.  Recently, she has filed a management plan for forest improvements, and will be filing a non-industrial timber harvest plan for future harvests.  Mother and I have worked together since the early 50s, and I have been both a laborer and a technical advisor to her for the years since 1953.  I generally devote about 3 days a week to the operation including weekends.

I am requesting a written opinion from your office on how to deal with the forestry decision, and further, if I should be entering some acknowledgment of the interest on my conflict statement.

Thank you for your help.

Sincerely,

[signature]
JAMES EDDIE
9401 Main Street
Potter Valley, CA  95469

sections.question_synthetic: A Mendocino County Board member seeks guidance on whether he has a conflict of interest regarding proposed timber harvest rules that may affect his mother's property (of which he is an heir) and whether he should disclose this interest on his conflict statement.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The sender (Eddie) is requesting a legal opinion from FPPC, not providing one. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present; the letter is narrative in form. The sender describes his family interest in properties that would be affected by proposed Mendocino County timber rules and asks for guidance on conflict of interest disclosure obligations.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: A Mendocino County Board member seeks guidance on whether he has a conflict of interest regarding proposed timber harvest rules that may affect his mother's property (of which he is an heir) and whether he should disclose this interest on his conflict statement.

embedding.summary: An incoming request letter from a Mendocino County Board member (Eddie) requesting a written legal opinion on whether he has a conflict of interest regarding proposed forestry rules affecting his mother's timber properties and whether he should acknowledge this interest on his conflict statement.
